disciplinary_practices.name: Engineering Design yo
disciplinary_practices.description: Supporting our young learners to use their knowledge and skills to figure out effective solutions to a given problem, just like engineers. The solution may involve the invention of a new product/ technology or improvements in existing ones.

steps.name: Test performance and optimize the product

pedagogical_approaches.name: Self-Directed Learning
pedagogical_approaches.description: Self-Directed Learning (SDL) is a popular educational method that encourages learning around real-world problems and empowers students. It uses a five-step structure, akin to problem-solving, comprising goal setting,self planning, self-monitoring, self-evaluation, and revision.

outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Empathize with users

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: What concepts have students discussed and developed in the forum?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you assess the depth of student understanding, the system analyzes forum posts for complexity, reasoning ability, and conceptual connections.

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: How have students' online learning interactions been at a particular learning stage with Forum?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you understand students’ online learning interactions at a particular stage, the system analyzes patterns of participation and communication in the Forum, revealing how students connect, collaborate, and engage with one another during that period.

learning_tasks.name: Students work on indentifying the most prominent problem needed to be addressed.

learning_tasks.name: Students present their findings on user needs to the whole class
A Task with a questionnaire

laqs_where_end_of_course_evaluation.question_text: How do students perceive their own learning by the end of the course?
laqs_where_end_of_course_evaluation.instruction: To help you understand how students perceive their overall learning by the end of the course and encourage meta-cognitive reflection, you can set up a survey to guide them in evaluating their own progress, challenges, and achievements, thereby increasing their awareness of learning strategies and outcomes.

curriculum_components.name: Through discussing essential design elements to ideate solution for self-planning

intended_learning_outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Ideate innovative solutions
Apply self-directed learning strategies (Self-planning) in the learning process

learning_tasks.name: Practice a range of technology/tools, and/or receive information to understand constraints and possibilities
Discuss in groups to identify essential design elements
Construct a visible artefact to represent design plans
Reflect on feedback through peer review process
Refine the plan

curriculum_components.name: Through collecting feedback to design solution for self-planning

intended_learning_outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Ideate innovative solutions
Apply self-directed learning strategies (Self-planning) in the learning process

learning_tasks.name: Students work out a plan/draw sketch design
Students receive feedback and comments from teachers and other students
Students revise the design problem based on the feedback received.

curriculum_components.name: Through creating criteria and material lists to construct prototype for self-monitoring

intended_learning_outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Build prototype
Apply self-directed learning strategies (Self-monitoring) in the learning process

learning_tasks.name: Group discussion to identify the factors to be evaluate the prototype that fulfills users' need
Students list criteria/rubric used to evaluate the effectiveness of the prototype
Students explores the materials
Students produce an early, inexpensive, and scaled down version of the product

curriculum_components.name: Through competition based on the rubrics to test performance of the product for self-evaluation

intended_learning_outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Test solution
Apply self-directed learning strategies (Self-evaluation) in the learning process

learning_tasks.name: Students receive teachers' instruction on the success criteria of the competition
Students present their product in the competition
Students self/peer evaluate the performance of their products based on the rubrics.

intended_learning_outcomes.statement: Apply design thinking process - Optimize solution
Apply self-directed learning strategies (Revision) in the learning process

disciplinary_practice_steps.name: Test performance and optimize the product

learning_tasks.name: Students analyze the feedback from users/teachers and search methods to optimise the product
Students work out a plan/draw sketch design to refine their production
Students work on the product optimization

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: What is the course completion status of each student?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you track each student's course completion status, you need to set completion conditions for every learning activity in iLAP. For example, you could require students to achieve a passing grade on a quiz.

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: To what extent have students participated in different tools of the iLAP course during school hours and after school?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you understand how actively students are learning during and after class, you can inform the Learning Analytics Facilitator of the in-class time period (e.g., 08:00 to 16:00 on weekdays).

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: How do students perform in the course?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you monitor student performance, some iLAP tools support grading features, such as Quiz, Forum, Workshop, H5P, and Assignment. You can use these tools for assessment and grading purposes.

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: How do students perceive their own learning by the end of the course?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you understand how students perceive their overall learning by the end of the course and encourage meta-cognitive reflection, you can set up a survey to guide them in evaluating their own progress, challenges, and achievements, thereby increasing their awareness of learning strategies and outcomes.

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: To what extent has each student participated in a specific task?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you understand how each student participated in a specific task, the system will provide the number of views and participation time for each activity to instructors for further analysis.

learning_analytic_questions.question_text: To what extent have students participated in the forum?
learning_analytic_questions.instruction: To help you analyze when students are most active in forums and measure their time management efficiency in forum participation, the system provides data on forum activity by hour.
